task.title: Enforce loop/session isolation policy
task.summary: Make compiler-loop fresh starts and gateway independence enforceable, and let adapters create bounded fresh-session handoffs when policy requires them.

goal.outcome: Compiler loops declare fresh-start policy, protected semantic validation gates block missing independence evidence, and adapters can initiate bounded fresh-session/context handoffs without manual user /new steps.
acceptance: Build policy can declare loop_start, validation, and next_loop isolation requirements.
Validation reports for profiles that require independence block when fresh-context/clean-worktree/sha evidence is missing.
Graph next-action/session metadata exposes when fresh session/context reset is required at loop handoff.
CodeWiki skills instruct agents to start next loops in fresh sessions or clear contexts and to keep validators independent from builder context.
Tests cover required isolation policy for build creation, validation blocking, and graph routing metadata.
CodeWiki knowledge defines adapter-neutral session handoff semantics for new-session, context-reset, and external-orchestrator modes.
Pi adapter registers a LLM-callable tool that stages a handoff and queues a command to call ctx.newSession with a minimal kickoff prompt.
Pi command handler uses replacement-session context only and sends the kickoff prompt after session replacement.
Agency/skills guidance tells agents to use the handoff primitive at required loop/session boundaries instead of asking the user to run /new.
Tests cover handoff payload creation/cleanup and command-string queuing behavior without needing an interactive Pi UI.
codewiki_claim supports registering a wait entry for desired scopes when a blocking write claim exists.
Wait entries have TTL, can be heartbeat-extended, can be cancelled/released, and expire if abandoned.
Claim release or expiry refreshes matching waiters from pending to ready when no blocking active claim remains.
Claim state/graph/status output exposes pending and ready waiters with blocked-by claim ids and source scopes.
Wake semantics remain artifact-first: ready waiters identify task/build/scopes for fresh resume rather than reviving stale chat context.
non_goals: Do not implement non-Pi harness adapters in this task.
Do not run unbounded autonomous sprint execution.
Do not bypass validation/claim/policy gates when spawning sessions.
Do not make every documentation or feedback gateway require a separate worktree by default.
Do not replace scoped claims or Git as coordination/publication truth.
No automatic release/steal of another session's claim.
No automatic session spawning in the first implementation.
No replacing existing claim conflict checks.
verification: Run npm run test:features or explain unrelated blockers.

exit_criteria: Fresh task-close validation before TASK-074 closure.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: TASK-074 changes code/tests and requires independent validation before closure.
evidence: Implementation build records checks and touched paths; clean validation still required for TASK-074 closure.
validation.handoff: Validate TASK-074 from build/task/spec artifacts in a clean worktree before closing.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence.

summary: Implemented claim wait queue support under TASK-074: wait action, waiter TTL/heartbeat/cancel, ready transitions on claim release/expiry, graph/status exposure, and smoke coverage.

evidence: tests/smoke/claim-wait-queue.test.mjs creates a blocking claim, registers a waiter, heartbeats WAIT-001, releases CLAIM-001, verifies WAIT-001 ready, then verifies graph waiter visibility.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Claim wait queue model added to shared types and application/claims.ts.
Graph/status/tool outputs extended to expose waiters.
Pi schema/action output updated for WAIT ids.
notes: TASK-074 was reused/refined by roadmap tool because wait queue overlaps existing loop/session isolation and handoff coordination work.
Automatic session spawning from ready waiters remains future work per accepted non-goal.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: tests/smoke/claim-wait-queue.test.mjs creates a blocking claim, registers a waiter, heartbeats WAIT-001, releases CLAIM-001, verifies WAIT-001 ready, then verifies graph waiter visibility.
code_change_evidence: Claim wait queue model added to shared types and application/claims.ts.
Graph/status/tool outputs extended to expose waiters.
Pi schema/action output updated for WAIT ids.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_claim supports registering a wait entry for desired scopes when a blocking write claim exists.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Added action='wait' to claim action schema/types; mutateChangeClaims creates WAIT-### entries with desired scopes and blocked_by_claim_ids.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Wait entries have TTL, can be heartbeat-extended, can be cancelled/released, and expire if abandoned.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Waiters carry expires_at/status; heartbeat accepts WAIT ids; release cancels WAIT ids; markExpired expires pending/ready waiters.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Claim release or expiry refreshes matching waiters from pending to ready when no blocking active claim remains.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: refreshWaiters runs after release/heartbeat/list and computes active blocking write claims; smoke test verifies release of CLAIM-001 makes WAIT-001 ready.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Claim state/graph/status output exposes pending and ready waiters with blocked-by claim ids and source scopes.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: buildChangeClaimState returns pending_waiter_count, ready_waiter_count, waiters; graph adds change_claim_waiter nodes and views.claims.waiters; status state includes waiter counts/list.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Wake semantics remain artifact-first: ready waiters identify task/build/scopes for fresh resume rather than reviving stale chat context.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Waiter records store task_id, build_ref, scopes, blocked_by_claim_ids, role/worktree metadata; docs state ready waiters are wake signals only.

risks: TASK-074 contains other pre-existing session-handoff changes; task-close validation should review full task scope in clean fresh context before closure.

restore.note: Restored history is reference material until promoted into active knowledge or roadmap truth.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-074 Enforce loop/session isolation policy implementation evidence
commit.body:
Implemented claim wait queue support under TASK-074: wait action, waiter TTL/heartbeat/cancel, ready transitions on claim release/expiry, graph/status exposure, and smoke coverage.

Checks: node tests/smoke/claim-wait-queue.test.mjs: pass, npm run typecheck: pass, npm run test:features: pass (10 tests), npm run test:smoke: pass, git diff --check scoped files: pass
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.

restore.note: Restored history is reference material until promoted into active knowledge or roadmap truth.

closure_brief.user_intent: Implement claim wait queue support so blocked agents can register waiters, heartbeat or cancel waits, and resume from current artifacts when blocking claims release or expire.
implemented_changes: Added claim waiter lifecycle behavior for pending, ready, cancelled, and expired waits.
Exposed wait queue state through graph/status surfaces.
Added smoke coverage for claim wait queue behavior.
acceptance_evidence: Implementation build records claim wait queue acceptance and smoke evidence.
remaining_risks: Historical build paths predate root src/ restructure and are retained as archival evidence.
